transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Good morning doctor. I have been having a headache and pain in my eyes.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Since when you have been experiencing this?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It started about two days ago.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Is the headache constant or does it come and go?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It's mostly constant but it gets worse when I look at the screens.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Where exactly is the headache located?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Mostly around my forehead and behind my eyes.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Do you have any other symptoms like fever, nausea or blood vision?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No fever but sometimes my vision feels slightly strained.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: How many hours do you spend on screen daily?

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: So that could be causing eye strain.

report_data.clinical_observations: Patient reports constant headache, worse with screen time, eye strain, and strained vision. No fever.

diagnosis.reasoning: Patient spends 8-10 hours on screen daily, reports eye strain and strained vision.
Patient reports constant headache, worse with screen time.
Patient has a pre-existing condition of Dust Allergy.

report_data.treatment_advice: Recommendations for reducing screen time, improving sleep habits, and using eye drops for eye strain.
report_data.action_plan: Follow-up in 2 days to assess improvement, consider referral to an eye specialist if symptoms persist.
report_data.verification_notes: Patient confirmed symptoms and medical history.
report_data.doctor_notes: Patient's symptoms are likely related to prolonged screen time and eye strain. Recommend lifestyle modifications and follow-up to assess improvement.
